title: enable --iidfile for podman-remote build
body:
for podman-remote build operations, the iidfile, when used, needs to write the file to the client's local filesystem.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@baude Code looks good, but I'm wondering if it should live in the bindings? Or even in the cmd tree vs. this deep in libraries?
i personally think this is fine ... if we have strong feelings, it can be moved.
Bindings feels a little wierd - they already pass back the ID, I don't think there's a need for them to also make a file with it.

Not thrilled but I guess our hands are forced.
/hold cancel
